standards.statement: Cite evidence to explain and justify reasoning.
Read and comprehend grade-level complex texts proficiently.
Make inferences to support comprehension.
Use appropriate collaborative techniques and active listening skills when engaging in discussions in a variety of situations.
Use the accepted rules governing a specific format to create quality work.
Use appropriate voice and tone when speaking or writing.
Demonstrate knowledge of the basic concepts of print.
Identify a printed word.
Distinguish letters from words.
Match print to receptive language to demonstrate that language is represented by print.
Identify parts of a book (front cover, back cover, title page) with a model.
Move left to right on the printed page.
Identify upper- and lowercase letters of the alphabet.
Recognize that print conveys specific meaning and pictures may support meaning.
Demonstrate phonological awareness.
Blend and segment syllables in spoken words.
Identify and produce alliterative and rhyming words.
Blend and segment onset and rimes of single-syllable words.
Identify the initial, medial and final sound of spoken words.
Add or delete phonemes at the beginning or end of a spoken word and produce the resulting word using the student's mode of communication.
Segment and blend phonemes in single-syllable spoken words.
Use knowledge of grade-appropriate phonics and word-analysis skills to decode words accurately.
Demonstrate knowledge of the most frequent sound for consonants.
Demonstrate knowledge of the short and long sounds for the five major vowels.
Decode consonant-vowel-consonant (CVC) words.
Encode consonant-vowel-consonant (CVC) words.
Recognize and read with automaticity grade-level high frequency words.
Recognize and read high frequency words.
Describe the main character(s), setting, and important events in a story.
Identify a character, a setting and an event in a story.
Explain the roles of author and illustrator of a story.
Identify the roles of author and illustrator of a story.
Identify rhyme in a poem.
Identify a rhyme.
Use titles, headings, and illustrations to predict and confirm the topic of texts.
Use titles and illustrations to predict and confirm the topic of texts.
Identify the topic of and multiple details in a text.
Identify the topic of and select a detail in a text.
Explain the difference between opinions and facts about a topic.
Identify an opinion or fact about a topic.
Identify and explain descriptive words in text(s).
Identify descriptive words in text(s).
Retell a text orally to enhance comprehension:
Identify main character, setting and important events for a story using the student's mode of communication.
Identify topic and details for an informational text using the student's mode of communication.
Compare and contrast characters’ experiences in stories.
Identify different character experiences in the same story.
Print many upper- and lowercase letters.
Print many upper- and lowercase letters with a model.
Using a combination of drawing, dictating, and/or writing, create narratives with the events in chronological order.
Using a combination of drawing, dictating and/or writing, create a narrative.
Using a combination of drawing, dictating, and/or writing, express opinions about a topic or text with at least one supporting reason.
Using a combination of drawing, dictating, selecting and/or writing, express an opinion about a topic.
Using a combination of drawing, dictating, and/or writing, provide factual information about a topic.
Using a combination of drawing, dictating, selecting and/or writing, provide a fact about a topic.
With guidance and support from adults, improve drawing and writing, as needed, by planning, revising, and editing.
With guidance and support from adults, improve drawing and writing, as needed, by planning and revising.
Present information orally using complete sentences.
Express information using complete sentences, using the student's mode of communication.
Follow the rules of standard English grammar, punctuation, capitalization, and spelling appropriate to grade level.
1 Follow the rules of standard English grammar, punctuation, capitalization and spelling.
Recall information to answer a question about a single topic.
Identify information to answer a question about a single topic.
Use a multimedia element to enhance oral or written tasks.
Identify the correct multimedia element to enhance oral or written tasks when presented with options.
Use grade-level academic vocabulary appropriately in speaking and writing.
Use grade-level academic vocabulary appropriately in communication, using the student's mode of communication.
Ask and answer questions about unfamiliar words in grade-level content.
Identify unfamiliar words in grade-level content at the student's ability level.
Identify and sort common words into basic categories, relating vocabulary to background knowledge.
Identify categories of common words in grade-level text at the student's ability level, using the student's mode of communication.
Locate the title, table of contents, names of author(s) and illustrator(s), and glossary of books.
Locate the title, table of contents, names of author(s) and illustrator(s), and glossary of books with a model.
Demonstrate phonological awareness.
Segment spoken words into initial, medial and final phonemes, including words with digraphs, blends and trigraphs.
Blend initial, medial and final phonemes together to identify or produce a single-syllable word that includes digraphs, blends or trigraphs.
Blend single-syllable spoken words with at least four phonemes.
Segment single-syllable spoken words with at least four phonemes.
Segment and blend phonemes in familiar multi-syllable spoken words.
Use knowledge of grade-appropriate phonics and word-analysis skills to decode words accurately.
Decode words using knowledge of spelling-sound correspondences for common consonant digraphs, trigraphs and blends.
Decode simple words with r-controlled vowels.
Decode and encode regularly spelled one-syllable words.
Decode words with inflectional endings.
Decode two-syllable words with regular patterns by breaking the words into syllables.
Decode words that use final -e and vowel teams to make long-vowel sound.
Read grade-level texts with accuracy, automaticity, and appropriate prosody or expression.
Recognize and read sight words.
Identify and describe the main story elements in a story.
Identify the main story elements in a story.
Identify and explain the moral of a story.
Identify the moral of a story.
Explain who is telling the story using context clues.
Identify who is telling the story using context clues.
Identify stanzas and line breaks in poems.
Identify a line break in a poem.
Use text features including titles, headings, captions, graphs, maps, glossaries, and/or illustrations to demonstrate understanding of texts.
Use text features including titles, headings and/or illustrations to demonstrate understanding of texts.
Identify the topic of and relevant details in a text.
Identify the topic of and select a relevant detail in a text.
Explain similarities and differences between information provided in visuals and words in an informational text.
Identify if information was provided from a visual or from words in an informational text.
Identify an author’s opinion(s) about the topic.
Identify an author's opinion about the topic.
Identify and explain descriptive words and phrases in text(s).
Identify descriptive words and phrases in text(s).
Retell a text in oral or written form to enhance comprehension.
Identify main story elements at the beginning, middle and end for a literary text using the student's mode of communication.
Identify a topic and relevant details for an informational text using the student's mode of communication.
Compare and contrast two texts on the same topic.
Identify details about two texts on the same topic.
Print all upper- and lowercase letters.
Print all upper- and lowercase letters with a model.
Write narratives that retell two or more appropriately sequenced events, including relevant details and a sense of closure.
Write a narrative with two sequenced events.
Write opinions about a topic or text with at least one supporting reason from a source and a sense of closure.
Write an opinion about a topic.
Write expository texts about a topic, using a source, providing facts and a sense of closure.
Write an expository text about a topic, using a source, to provide facts.
With guidance and support from adults, improve writing, as needed, by planning, revising, and editing.
With guidance and support from adults, improve drawing and writing, as needed, by planning, revising and editing.
Present information orally using complete sentences and appropriate volume.
Express information using complete sentences and appropriate volume, using the student's mode of communication.
Follow the rules of standard English grammar, punctuation, capitalization, and spelling appropriate to grade level.
Follow the rules of standard English grammar, punctuation, capitalization and spelling.
Participate in research to gather information to answer a question about a single topic.
Participate in guided research to gather information to answer a question about a single topic.
Use a multimedia element to enhance oral or written tasks.
Identify the correct multimedia element to enhance oral or written tasks when presented with options.
Identify and use digital tools to produce and publish writing individually or with peers and with support from adults.
Identify digital tools to produce and publish writing individually or with peers and with support from adults.
Use grade-level academic vocabulary appropriately in speaking and writing.
Identify grade-level academic vocabulary appropriately in communication, using the student's mode of communication.
Identify and use frequently occurring base words and their common inflections in grade-level content.
Identify frequently occurring base words and their common inflections in grade-level content at the student's ability level.
Identify and use picture clues, context clues, word relationships, reference materials, and/or background knowledge to determine the meaning of unknown words.
Identify and use picture clues, context clues and/or background knowledge to determine the meaning of unknown words at the student's ability level.
Use knowledge of grade-appropriate phonics and word-analysis skills to decode words.
Decode words with variable vowel teams (e.g., oo, ea, ou) and vowel diphthongs (e.g., oi, oy, ow).
Decode regularly spelled two-syllable words with long and short vowels.
Decode words with open (e.g., hi, baby, moment) and closed (e.g., bag, sunshine, chop) syllables and consonant -le (e.g., purple, circle, stumble).
Decode words with common prefixes and suffixes.
Decode words with silent letter combinations (e.g., knight, comb, island, ghost).
Read grade-level texts with accuracy, automaticity, and appropriate prosody or expression.
Write an expository text about a topic, using a source, providing an introduction and facts.
Identify plot structure and describe main story elements in a literary text.
Sequence and describe main elements in a literary text.
Identify and explain a theme of a literary text.
Identify the theme of a literary text.
Identify different characters’ perspectives in a literary text.
Match characters and their perspectives in a literary text.
Identify rhyme schemes in poems.
Identify a rhyme scheme in a poem.
Explain how text features - including titles, headings, captions, graphs, maps, glossaries, and/or illustrations - contribute to the meaning of texts.
Show how text features—including titles, headings, graphs, maps and/or illustrations—contribute to the meaning of texts.
Identify the central idea and relevant details in a text.
Identify the central idea and relevant details in a text.
Explain an author’s purpose in an informational text.
Identify an author's purpose in an informational text.
Explain an author’s opinion(s) and supporting evidence.
Identify an author's opinion and supporting evidence.
Identify and explain similes, idioms, and alliteration in text(s).
Identify similes, idioms and alliteration in text(s).
Retell a text to enhance comprehension.
Identify main story elements and sequence relevant details in a logical order for a literary text using the student's mode of communication.
Identify the central idea and a relevant detail for an informational text using the student's mode of communication.
Compare and contrast important details presented by two texts on the same topic or theme.
Compare the important details presented by two texts on the same topic or theme.
Demonstrate legible printing skills.
Write letters and/or groups of letters with adequate spacing.
Write personal or fictional narratives using a logical sequence of events, transitions, and an ending.
Write a narrative that includes a beginning, middle and end.
Write opinions about a topic or text with reasons supported by details from a source, use transitions, and provide a conclusion.
Write an opinion about a topic with one supporting reason.
Write expository texts about a topic, using a source, providing an introduction, facts, transitions, and a conclusion.
Write an expository text about a topic, using a source, providing an introduction and facts.
Improve writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing with guidance and support from adults and feedback from peers.
Improve writing as needed by planning, revising and editing, with guidance and support from adults.
Present information orally using complete sentences, appropriate volume, and clear pronunciation.
Express information using complete sentences and appropriate volume, using the student's mode of communication.
Follow the rules of standard English grammar, punctuation, capitalization, and spelling appropriate to grade level.
Follow the rules of standard English grammar, punctuation, capitalization and spelling.
Participate in research to gather information to answer a question about a single topic using multiple sources.
Participate in guided research to gather information to answer a question about a single topic using multiple sources.
Use one or more multimedia element(s) to enhance oral or written tasks.
Identify one multimedia element to enhance oral or written tasks
Use digital tools to produce and publish writing individually or with peers and with support from adults.
Identify and use digital tools to produce and publish writing individually or with peers and with support from adults.
Use grade-level academic vocabulary appropriately in speaking and writing.
Identify grade-level academic vocabulary appropriately in communication, using the student's mode of communication.
Identify and use base words and affixes to determine the meaning of unfamiliar words in grade-level content.
2 Identify base words and affixes to determine the meaning of unfamiliar words in grade-level content at the student's ability level.
Identify and use context clues, word relationships, reference materials, and/or background knowledge to determine the meaning of unknown words.
Identify and use picture clues, context clues, word relationships and/or background knowledge to determine the meaning of unknown words at the student's ability level.
Use knowledge of grade-level phonics and word-analysis skills to decode words.
Decode words with common Greek and Latin roots and affixes (see 3.V.1.2).
Decode words with common derivational suffixes and describe how they turn words into different parts of speech (e.g., -ful, -less, -est).
Decode multisyllabic words.
Read grade-level texts with accuracy, automaticity, and appropriate prosody or expression.
Read grade-level texts, at the student's ability level, with accuracy and expression using the student's mode of communication.
Explain how one or more characters develop throughout the plot in a literary text.
Identify how a character develops throughout the plot in a literary text.
Explain a theme and how it develops, using details, in a literary text.
Identify a theme and how it develops, using details, in a literary text.
Explain different characters’ perspectives in a literary text.
Identify different characters' perspectives in a literary text.
Identify types of poems: free verse, rhymed verse, haiku, and limerick.
Identify poems with rhyme and poems without rhyme.
Explain how text features contribute to meaning and identify the text structures of chronology, comparison, and cause/effect in texts.
Identify the text structures of chronological order, comparison and cause/effect in texts.
Identify the central idea and explain how relevant details support that idea in a text.
Identify the central idea and select relevant details that supports that idea in a text.
Explain the development of an author's purpose in an informational text.
Identify what evidence is included in an informational text that develops the author's purpose.
Identify an author’s claim and explain how an author uses evidence to support the claim.
Identify an author's claim and evidence used to support the claim.
Identify and explain metaphors, personification, and hyperbole in text(s).
Identify metaphors, personification and hyperbole in text(s).
Summarize a text to enhance comprehension.
Identify the plot for a literary text using the student's mode of communication.
Identify the central idea and relevant details for an informational text using the student's mode of communication.
Compare and contrast how two authors present information on the same topic or theme.
Compare and contrast important information presented by two authors on the same topic or theme.
Write in cursive all upper- and lowercase letters.
Write cursive letters with a model.
Write personal or fictional narratives using a logical sequence of events, appropriate descriptions, dialogue, a variety of transitional words or phrases, and an ending.
Write personal or fictional narratives using a logical sequence of events, appropriate details and an ending.
Write opinions about a topic or text, include reasons supported by details from one or more sources, use transitions, and provide a conclusion.
Write an opinion about a topic with one supporting reason and a conclusion.
Write expository texts about a topic, using one or more sources, providing an introduction, facts and details, some elaboration, transitions, and a conclusion.
Write an expository text about a topic, using a source, providing an introduction, facts and a conclusion.
Improve writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing with guidance and support from adults and feedback from peers.
Improve writing as needed by planning, revising and editing with guidance, support and modeling from adults and feedback from peers.
Present information orally, in a logical sequence, using nonverbal cues, appropriate volume, and clear pronunciation.
Express information in a logical sequence, using nonverbal cues, using the student's mode of communication.
Follow the rules of standard English grammar, punctuation, capitalization, and spelling appropriate to grade level.
Follow the rules of standard English grammar, punctuation, capitalization and spelling.
Conduct research to answer a question, organizing information about the topic from multiple sources.
Participate in research to answer a question, organizing information about the topic from multiple sources.
Use two or more multimedia elements to enhance oral or written tasks.
Identify one or more multimedia elements to enhance oral and written tasks.
Use digital writing tools individually or collaboratively to plan, draft, and revise writing.
Use digital writing tools individually or collaboratively to draft writing with support from adults.
Use grade-level academic vocabulary appropriately in speaking and writing.
Identify and use grade-level academic vocabulary appropriately in communication, using the student's mode of communication.

Identify and use common Greek and Latin roots, base words, and affixes to determine the meaning of unfamiliar words in grade-level content at the student's ability level.

Use context clues, figurative language, word relationships, reference materials, and/or background knowledge to determine the meaning of multiple-meaning and unknown words and phrases, appropriate to grade level.

Use knowledge of grade-level phonics and word-analysis skills to decode words.
